creator: sobomax@FreeBSD.org
text:
Akinori MUSHA wrote:

> At Wed, 5 Sep 2001 14:30:13 +0200 (CEST),
> Volker Stolz wrote:
> > None yet, I don't know who's telling rep-gtk to use the path containing
> > the 4.3.
> > Workarounds:
> > 1) Symlink /usr/X11R6/libexec/rep/i386--freebsd4.3 to
> >       /usr/X11R6/libexec/rep/i386--freebsd4.4 (tested)
> > 2) Reinstall entire Gnome/rep-gtk/sawfish (not tested)
>
> What about configuring the rep related ports with:
>
>         CONFIGURE_TARGET=${ARCH}-freebsd${OSREL:C/\..*//}
>
> I've recently changed the Ruby ports that way to eliminate the
> upgrading problem.

Uh, huh. This is possible, but it would distract users, as they will need to
recompile/reinstall librep, rep-gtk and Sawfish to convert to the new world
order. I'm not sure that it worth it.

Thank you for the tip, though.

-Maxim
